data.title: Merck Weighs Revolution Medicines Buyout And Expands AI Oncology Bet
data.description:
• Merck (NYSE:MRK) is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire Revolution Medicines in one of the largest biotech deals in recent years.
  • The potential acquisition would expand Merck's oncology pipeline, with a focus on targeted cancer therapies.
  • Merck is also a founding partner in Illumina's new AI driven pharmacogenomics initiative.
  • Recent collaborations with ChemLex and Guardant Health point to a broader push into AI enabled drug discovery and precision oncology.

For investors, this cluster of moves suggests that Merck is leaning further into oncology and data heavy R&D. The company already has a significant presence in cancer treatments, and adding Revolution Medicines together with AI and genomics partnerships could influence how its future pipeline is built.

These developments may affect how investors think about Merck's long term growth drivers, its capital allocation priorities, and its positioning relative to other large drugmakers. An important question will be how effectively Merck converts these deals and collaborations into clinically meaningful drugs and viable new revenue streams.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Merck by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Merck.

Why Merck could be great value

For investors, this cluster of deals points to Merck leaning harder into targeted oncology and data heavy R&D, with the possible US$28b to US$32b Revolution Medicines buyout sitting alongside AI, genomics and automation alliances as part of the same theme. If these talks progress, the price paid, the structure of any earn outs, and how Revolution’s RAS focused pipeline is slotted next to Merck’s existing oncology assets will likely shape how the market views management’s capital discipline and long term growth ambitions.

Advertisement

Merck Narrative, Reinforced Or Stretched?

Recent commentary around Merck has focused on building an oncology portfolio that can support growth as key products mature, and this move fits squarely into that story of heavy investment in cancer, data and platforms. For anyone following the Merck narrative as a free cash flow generator that can fund sizable deals while maintaining dividends, this potential acquisition and the Illumina, Guardant and ChemLex tie ups give more concrete evidence of where management is choosing to lean in.

Risks and rewards investors should weigh

  • Multiple alliances with Illumina, Guardant and ChemLex point to a broad push into AI, genomics and automation that could widen Merck’s future oncology and precision medicine toolkit.
  • A Revolution Medicines deal, if completed, would add late stage targeted cancer programs that are aligned with Merck’s existing oncology focus.
  • A US$28b to US$32b acquisition would be a large use of capital, so investors will likely scrutinize integration risk and whether returns ultimately justify the outlay.
  • Heavy reliance on external partnerships and a big biotech deal increases execution risk across clinical, regulatory and commercial stages.

What to watch next

From here, the key signals are whether Merck confirms exclusive terms with Revolution Medicines, how it outlines financial and pipeline impacts if a deal is agreed, and what it shares about timelines and early data from the Illumina, Guardant and ChemLex collaborations on upcoming calls and presentations. data.title: Hims And Hers Wegovy Talks Could Reshape Growth And Subscriber Economics
data.description:
• Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) is in discussions with Novo Nordisk to add Wegovy injections to its telehealth platform.
  • The talks also include potential access to an oral version of Wegovy for weight management.
  • The move would introduce a new category of prescription weight loss treatments to the company’s existing offerings.

Hims & Hers Health runs a direct-to-consumer telehealth and subscriptions model focused on areas like hair loss, mental health, skincare, and sexual health. Bringing a branded weight management drug such as Wegovy into that mix would push the business into a higher profile treatment category that has drawn interest from consumers and healthcare providers.

For you as an investor, a key question is how a potential Novo Nordisk partnership could shape the product mix, unit economics, and brand perception of NYSE:HIMS over time. Access to Wegovy could attract a different type of customer and may influence factors such as marketing spend and retention patterns, so it is an area worth watching as details emerge.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Hims & Hers Health by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Hims & Hers Health.

How Hims & Hers Health stacks up against its biggest competitors

For Hims & Hers, access to Wegovy would push the company further into high-demand prescription care where branded drugs and medical oversight are central, rather than only lifestyle or generic offerings. That could deepen engagement with existing subscribers and potentially support higher average revenue per user, but it would also likely come with tighter clinical protocols and dependency on a single large pharma partner for supply and pricing terms.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Hims & Hers Health Narrative

The stock already attracts attention for its growth potential, premium pricing and focus on younger consumers, and Wegovy talks feed into a narrative of Hims & Hers trying to broaden its role in prescription care. At the same time, recent commentary about slowing growth, tougher competition in men’s sexual health, and heavy marketing investment means investors may view this development as one piece of a much bigger execution story rather than a quick fix.

Risks and Rewards To Keep In Mind

  • Access to a well known weight management drug could widen the addressable market on the platform and support subscriber growth.
  • If integrated well, weight loss treatments might complement existing categories and help spread fixed marketing and tech costs across more revenue streams.
  • Dependence on a branded product from Novo Nordisk could expose Hims & Hers to supply constraints, pricing changes or contract renegotiation risk.
  • Entering prescription weight loss at scale may require higher clinical, operational and regulatory spend, which could pressure margins if uptake or pricing disappoint.

What To Watch Next

From here, the key things to watch are whether Hims & Hers secures concrete terms with Novo Nordisk, how any Wegovy rollout is priced and marketed on the platform, and how that filters through to profit margins and subscriber trends versus the pressure already flagged in other categories. data.title: BYD Balances Cost Leadership With Premium Models And Margin Constraints
data.description:
• BYD is keeping a strong focus on cost leadership and accessible pricing, which is structurally limiting its profit margins.
  • This holds true even as the company pushes into higher-end segments with premium models such as the Yangwang series.
  • The setup suggests BYD may rely more on scale and broader revenue streams than on luxury level margins from premium vehicles.

For investors watching BYD (SEHK:1211), the key tension is clear: the company is positioning itself as a volume leader while experimenting with premium offerings. The current share price of HK$97.0 sits alongside a mixed return profile, with gains of 5.2% over 1 year and 32.6% over 3 years, but a 1.8% decline year to date. That mix underlines how the market is still working through what this business model means for long term profitability.

Looking ahead, the important question is not just whether Yangwang or other premium lines gain traction, but how much they actually move the margin needle. If cost leadership continues to define pricing, investors may want to think about BYD's earnings power as driven more by scale, efficiency and gradual diversification than by a sharp shift to luxury level profitability.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for BYD by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on BYD.

Why BYD could be great value

For investors, the key takeaway from this update is that BYD appears to be reinforcing a volume driven model where affordable pricing is central, even as it introduces higher end brands like Yangwang. That can appeal to buyers who see the company as a scale focused manufacturer, but it also signals that margin expansion from premium models may be more incremental than transformational for the group.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The BYD Narrative

This pricing stance feeds directly into the existing story many investors follow, which is BYD as a cost focused producer building earnings on size, vertical integration and broad product reach rather than luxury positioning. The Yangwang and software push can still contribute higher margin pockets, but this news suggests those are likely to sit on top of, not replace, the core mass market approach.

Risks and Rewards To Keep In Mind

  • ⚠️ High reliance on cost leadership may leave BYD more exposed if input costs rise or price competition intensifies.
  • ⚠️ A structurally capped margin profile could limit upside for investors who are hoping for luxury level profitability.
  • 🎁 Focus on accessible pricing can help support volume and factory utilization across cycles.
  • 🎁 Gradual growth in premium and software related revenue streams offers scope to lift overall earnings quality over time.

What To Watch Next

From here, it is worth tracking how much premium and software features contribute to BYD's overall profitability mix, and how the market reacts to that balance between scale and margin. If you want to see how other investors are framing this trade off in real time, check out the discussions in the Community Narratives section.

data.title: Is There An Opportunity With Sunrise Communications AG's (VTX:SUNN) 28% Undervaluation?
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • Using the 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity, Sunrise Communications fair value estimate is CHF55.70
  • Sunrise Communications is estimated to be 28% undervalued based on current share price of CHF40.00
  • The CHF47.32 analyst price target for SUNN is 15% less than our estimate of fair value

In this article we are going to estimate the intrinsic value of Sunrise Communications AG (VTX:SUNN) by taking the forecast future cash flows of the company and discounting them back to today's value. The Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model is the tool we will apply to do this. There's really not all that much to it, even though it might appear quite complex.

Companies can be valued in a lot of ways, so we would point out that a DCF is not perfect for every situation. Anyone interested in learning a bit more about intrinsic value should have a read of the Simply Wall St analysis model.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

The Model

We're using the 2-stage growth model, which simply means we take in account two stages of company's growth. In the initial period the company may have a higher growth rate and the second stage is usually assumed to have a stable growth rate. In the first stage we need to estimate the cash flows to the business over the next ten years. Where possible we use analyst estimates, but when these aren't available we extrapolate the previous free cash flow (FCF) from the last estimate or reported value. We assume companies with shrinking free cash flow will slow their rate of shrinkage, and that companies with growing free cash flow will see their growth rate slow, over this period. We do this to reflect that growth tends to slow more in the early years than it does in later years.

Generally we assume that a dollar today is more valuable than a dollar in the future, and so the sum of these future cash flows is then discounted to today's value:

10-year free cash flow (FCF) forecast

2026202720282029203020312032203320342035
Levered FCF (CHF, Millions) CHF372.7mCHF374.1mCHF358.2mCHF281.5mCHF281.5mCHF260.3mCHF247.0mCHF238.6mCHF233.2mCHF229.8m
Growth Rate Estimate SourceAnalyst x5Analyst x5Analyst x3Analyst x1Analyst x1Est @ -7.51%Est @ -5.11%Est @ -3.43%Est @ -2.26%Est @ -1.43%
Present Value (CHF, Millions) Discounted @ 6.8% CHF349CHF328CHF294CHF217CHF203CHF176CHF156CHF141CHF129CHF119

("Est" = FCF growth rate estimated by Simply Wall St)
Present Value of 10-year Cash Flow (PVCF) = CHF2.1b

The second stage is also known as Terminal Value, this is the business's cash flow after the first stage. For a number of reasons a very conservative growth rate is used that cannot exceed that of a country's GDP growth. In this case we have used the 5-year average of the 10-year government bond yield (0.5%) to estimate future growth. In the same way as with the 10-year 'growth' period, we discount future cash flows to today's value, using a cost of equity of 6.8%.

Terminal Value (TV)= FCF2035 × (1 + g) ÷ (r – g) = CHF230m× (1 + 0.5%) ÷ (6.8%– 0.5%) = CHF3.7b

Present Value of Terminal Value (PVTV)= TV / (1 + r)10= CHF3.7b÷ ( 1 + 6.8%)10= CHF1.9b

The total value is the sum of cash flows for the next ten years plus the discounted terminal value, which results in the Total Equity Value, which in this case is CHF4.0b. In the final step we divide the equity value by the number of shares outstanding. Compared to the current share price of CHF40.0, the company appears a touch undervalued at a 28% discount to where the stock price trades currently. The assumptions in any calculation have a big impact on the valuation, so it is better to view this as a rough estimate, not precise down to the last cent.

The Assumptions

We would point out that the most important inputs to a discounted cash flow are the discount rate and of course the actual cash flows. You don't have to agree with these inputs, I recommend redoing the calculations yourself and playing with them. The DCF also does not consider the possible cyclicality of an industry, or a company's future capital requirements, so it does not give a full picture of a company's potential performance. Given that we are looking at Sunrise Communications as potential shareholders, the cost of equity is used as the discount rate, rather than the cost of capital (or weighted average cost of capital, WACC) which accounts for debt. In this calculation we've used 6.8%, which is based on a levered beta of 1.490. Beta is a measure of a stock's volatility, compared to the market as a whole. We get our beta from the industry average beta of globally comparable companies, with an imposed limit between 0.8 and 2.0, which is a reasonable range for a stable business.

Strength
  • Debt is well covered by cash flow.
  • Dividend is in the top 25% of dividend payers in the market.
    Dividend information for SUNN.
Weakness
  • Interest payments on debt are not well covered.
    What are analysts forecasting for SUNN?
Opportunity
  • Forecast to reduce losses next year.
  • Has sufficient cash runway for more than 3 years based on current free cash flows.
  • Good value based on P/S ratio and estimated fair value.
Threat
  • No apparent threats visible for SUNN.

Next Steps:

Although the valuation of a company is important, it shouldn't be the only metric you look at when researching a company. The DCF model is not a perfect stock valuation tool. Preferably you'd apply different cases and assumptions and see how they would impact the company's valuation. For example, changes in the company's cost of equity or the risk free rate can significantly impact the valuation. Can we work out why the company is trading at a discount to intrinsic value? What's The Estimated Valuation?

As Vantage Towers operates in the telecom sector, we need to calculate the intrinsic value slightly differently. In this approach dividends per share (DPS) are used, as free cash flow is difficult to estimate and often not reported by analysts. This often underestimates the value of a stock, but it can still be good as a comparison to competitors. The 'Gordon Growth Model' is used, which simply assumes that dividend payments will continue to increase at a sustainable growth rate forever. For a number of reasons a very conservative growth rate is used that cannot exceed that of a company's Gross Domestic Product (GDP). Value Per Share = Expected Dividend Per Share / (Discount Rate - Perpetual Growth Rate)

= €1.5 / (4.9% – 1.6%)

= €43.3

The Assumptions

The calculation above is very dependent on two assumptions. The first is the discount rate and the other is the cash flows. You don't have to agree with these inputs, I recommend redoing the calculations yourself and playing with them. The DCF also does not consider the possible cyclicality of an industry, or a company's future capital requirements, so it does not give a full picture of a company's potential performance. Given that we are looking at Vantage Towers as potential shareholders, the cost of equity is used as the discount rate, rather than the cost of capital (or weighted average cost of capital, WACC) which accounts for debt. data.title: Union Pacific Merger Setback Highlights Rising Rail Regulatory And Investor Risks
data.description:
• Federal regulators rejected the initial Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern merger application.
  • The Surface Transportation Board requested a revised filing with additional information.
  • Analysts reacted with downgrades, citing higher uncertainty around the proposed deal.

For investors watching NYSE:UNP, this decision places one of the largest US freight rail operators in a holding pattern on a major deal. Union Pacific is a core player in long haul freight, and any change in its network or competitive position can matter for volumes, pricing, and capital plans. The regulator’s pushback highlights how closely rail consolidation is being reviewed at this time.

From here, the focus is on how Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern adjust their filing and timetable, and whether they modify deal terms or integration plans. The outcome is uncertain, and the process can influence how management prioritizes balance sheet flexibility, network investments, and potential alternatives if the merger case proves difficult to advance.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Union Pacific by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Union Pacific.

Is Union Pacific financially strong enough to weather the next crisis?

The Surface Transportation Board’s rejection of the first merger filing keeps Union Pacific’s largest potential deal in a holding pattern and introduces more uncertainty around timing, costs, and possible conditions. The regulators’ request for detailed market share projections and missing merger documents suggests the review may be data heavy and could require management attention that might otherwise go to network projects and shareholder returns.

Advertisement

Union Pacific’s Narrative Meets a Tougher Regulator

Union Pacific is often talked about as a steady, real economy infrastructure play with strong cash generation and reliable dividends, so a large merger was already a big swing compared with its usual slow burn story. The setback on the filing may reinforce the view that the core appeal still lies in the existing western US network and cash flows, rather than in transformational deals that depend on regulatory approvals.

Balancing Risks and Rewards After the STB Pushback

  • ⚠️ Regulatory risk has clearly risen, with a key ruling already putting the merger at “a little more risk” and raising questions over possible concessions or delays.
  • ⚠️ Management could face higher legal, advisory, and lobbying costs, alongside distraction from projects like the planned Mainline Texas Industrial Park.
  • 🎁 If the merger eventually proceeds, clearer data on market share and truck to rail shifts could support a more transparent case for long term freight volumes and efficiency.
  • 🎁 Even without the deal, Union Pacific’s role as a core freight rail operator with a long dividend track record continues to underpin its appeal for long horizon investors.

What to Watch Next

From here, the key things to track are how quickly Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern resubmit, what additional disclosures they provide on market share and competitive impact, and whether regulators hint at specific conditions or structural remedies.
